metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the Jira-keyed baseline (`arbitration_prev` from `get_baseline()`) is canonicalized before the new neutral `_local_matches_prev`/`_jira_matches_prev` helpers compare against it — either by calling `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(baseline)` at the top of the new comparison function, or by storing baselines in canonical shape — and add a characterization test case that exercises directionality suppression with a real baseline present.
evidence: binding_store.py:71-77: `_BASELINE_FIELDS = ("summary", "description", "priority", "status", "assignee")` — Jira-keyed, raw Jira values
outbound_fields.py:416-418: `arbitration_prev = binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)` — used directly in _local_matches_prev and _jira_matches_prev

outbound_fields.py:467-470: directionality suppression calls `_local_matches_prev(field_name, local_val, arbitration_prev or {})` where `field_name` is currently Jira-keyed (from `_map_local_to_jira_fields()`) — after the plan, `field_name` becomes local-keyed but `arbitration_prev` remains Jira-keyed
outbound_fields.py:284-286: `_local_matches_prev` calls `_extract_jira_field(prev_jira_fields, field_name)` — after change, would look up canonical key `"title"` in a dict that only has `"summary"`
Plan Approach §2: 'new neutral field-comparison helpers in core (local vocabulary only)' — baseline shape mismatch not addressed
findings.finding: The per-binding baseline (`arbitration_prev` = `binding_store.get_baseline()`) is stored in Jira field names and shapes (`_BASELINE_FIELDS = ("summary", "description", "priority", "status", "assignee")` with raw Jira nested values). After the plan canonicalizes the comparison loop to local field names, the new neutral `_local_matches_prev` and `_jira_matches_prev` equivalents will be called with canonical field names (e.g. `"title"`, not `"summary"`) against a baseline that has no `"title"` key — silently making all baseline lookups return empty/None and degrading directionality suppression to unconditional local-wins for every ticket on every pass. The conflict-detection path (`_jira_matches_prev`) has the same mismatch: it would receive the canonicalized snapshot but still call `_extract_jira_field` (which expects Jira-nested `{name: ...}` dicts) on it. The plan does not specify how this shape mismatch is resolved.
findings.location: Approach §2 (neutral field-comparison helpers); Approach §4 (InboundMapper widening); Testing §Characterization (inbound-directionality suppression)
scenarios: After migration, every pass: title/summary directionality suppression silently misses (baseline key mismatch) → every ticket whose local title matches Jira will emit a spurious outbound update reverting any Jira-side title edits rather than letting inbound mirror them
Conflict detection for all five mirrored fields silently disables → `conflict_sink` never fires, hiding concurrent-edit collisions that the a713 fix was designed to surface
findings.suggested_fix: At the point where `arbitration_prev` is obtained from `binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)`, canonicalize it immediately: `arbitration_prev_canonical = inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(arbitration_prev) if arbitration_prev else None`. All subsequent neutral helper calls use the canonical form. This is a single additional `map_remote_to_local` call, consistent with the plan's step-2 pattern, and keeps the baseline-storage format unchanged (no binding_store migration needed).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address the `_BASELINE_FIELDS` / `get_baseline()` shape in the plan: either (a) canonicalize the baseline to local keys and update `binding_store._BASELINE_FIELDS`, `set_baseline()` call-sites, and the neutral `_local_matches_prev`-equivalent accordingly, or (b) document that the new neutral helpers accept a Jira-shaped baseline and explain how field-name mapping ('summary'↔'title') is handled without re-introducing vendor reads in core.
evidence: _BASELINE_FIELDS = ('summary', 'description', 'priority', 'status', 'assignee') — Jira-keyed, not local-keyed ('title' is missing; 'summary' is used)
_jira_matches_prev calls _extract_jira_field(jira_fields, field_name) and _extract_jira_field(prev_jira_fields, field_name) — vendor-shape reads on both current and baseline
_local_matches_prev at outbound_fields.py:284 calls _extract_jira_field(prev_jira_fields, field_name) — baseline is vendor-shaped
Plan §2 and §6 describe 'core policies ... expressed on local field names' but do not mention baseline canonicalization or _BASELINE_FIELDS format change
findings.finding: The conflict-detection path (`_jira_matches_prev`, `outbound_fields.py:290-308`) compares raw Jira-shaped `jira_fields` AND `arbitration_prev` (from `get_baseline()`, which stores Jira-keyed fields: 'summary', 'description', 'priority', 'status', 'assignee'). The plan proposes neutral helpers operating in canonical/local shape but does not address that `arbitration_prev` remains Jira-shaped — the new helpers either cannot be truly neutral or the baseline format must change (an unlisted scope item). This risks silently dropping conflict detection on the new path.
scenarios: If neutral helpers compare canonical-mapped current snapshot against the Jira-keyed baseline, field-name mismatches ('title' vs 'summary') cause the directionality suppression and conflict-detection guards to silently no-op — phantom outbound updates resume.
If the baseline format must change to local keys, set_baseline() call-sites (reconcile.py:520, apply_inbound_records.py:211) need updating — unlisted scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate in Scope/Approach where `_parent_clear_is_managed` logic migrates: either as a new neutral core helper in `outbound_differ.py` (taking BindingStore) or retained in `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py` and called via the port, and confirm its contract is preserved in the characterization suite.
evidence: Plan §4: 'the remote parent id (from fields.parent.key), so core compares the BindingStore-resolved expected remote key against the mapped remote key, replacing outbound_fields.py:531-558'
outbound_fields.py:547: 'and not _parent_clear_is_managed(jira_parent_key, ticket, binding_store)' — gate that prevents clearing a human-set Jira parent
_parent_clear_is_managed is defined only in adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py:311 with no equivalent in core
Plan Scope does not list a new core function or explain migration of this gate
findings.finding: The managed-parent-clear gate `_parent_clear_is_managed` (adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py:311, called at :547) implements logic currently inside `_diff_fields`. The plan replaces the parent diff with canonical comparison (Approach §4: 'replacing outbound_fields.py:531-558'), but does not enumerate where the managed-parent-clear logic migrates or what its neutral-core equivalent looks like. Without this, the parent-clear managed-ref semantics are silently dropped.
scenarios: If _parent_clear_is_managed logic is silently dropped during the rewrite, the new core parent diff would clear Jira parents that were set directly in Jira (not managed by rebar), clobbering human-assigned parents instead of adopting them inbound — a behavioral regression.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit specification that the `inbound_fields.py` mapper widening uses NEW output keys (e.g. `assignee_aliases`, `parent_remote_key`, `reporter_account_id`) distinct from the existing `assignee` string key, or document that the inbound differ's consumption path at `inbound_differ.py:159-245` is simultaneously updated to remain correct.
evidence: Current `_map_jira_to_local_fields` returns `assignee` as a single extracted string (line 163: `assignee = _extract_jira_field_value(jira_fields, 'assignee') or ''`), with no `parent` or `reporter` key.
`inbound_differ.py:180-188` already has its own `_assignee_matches` call operating on the raw Jira dict passed separately — not via the mapper output.

The inbound differ at line 159 passes the FULL raw jira_fields to `map_remote_to_local` and then uses `jira_mapped` for field comparisons; adding structured alias dicts to the output could break the inbound differ's string comparisons.
No backward-compat note or inbound-path impact analysis appears anywhere in the plan text.
findings.finding: The plan's step 4 widens `_map_jira_to_local_fields` to add assignee identity aliases (all three of displayName / emailAddress / accountId), parent remote id, and reporter aliases to the mapper output — but the inbound differ already consumes this same mapper at `inbound_differ.py:159` and then directly accesses `jira_mapped` fields (e.g. `jira_mapped.get('assignee')` at line 180, `jira_mapped.get('parent_id')` at line 239). The plan does not specify that the widened output remains backward-safe for the existing inbound consumption path, which expects `assignee` as a plain string and has no `parent` or `reporter` key handling.
scenarios: If `assignee` is changed from a string to a dict-of-aliases in the mapper output, the inbound differ's `local_assignee == jira_mapped.get('assignee')` comparison (or its _assignee_matches call) would receive a dict instead of a string, producing phantom inbound mutations on every pass.
If `parent` is added to the mapper output, the inbound differ's parent handling at lines 234-246 (which currently reads raw `jira_fields` via `_extract_parent_local_id`) may double-process the field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name the canonical key for reporter in the InboundMapper widening (step 4) and specify how the replacement for `_diff_reporter` reads only from the canonical snapshot — confirming no `jira_fields.get("reporter").get("accountId")` pattern survives in core after the migration.
evidence: outbound_fields.py:356-366: `_diff_reporter` reads `jira_fields.get("reporter").get("accountId")` — direct Jira vendor key access
Plan step 4: 'reporter identity aliases for the outbound one-way reporter diff' — lists what to add to InboundMapper but not the canonical key name nor how _diff_reporter's vendor read is replaced
Plan step 2: 'no core code reads vendor snapshot keys (status.name, accountId, parent.key, ADF)' — reporter.accountId is not in this list but is equally a vendor key
findings.finding: The plan says step 2 eliminates all vendor key reads from core ('no core code reads vendor snapshot keys') but does not fully specify how `_diff_reporter`'s vendor read is replaced. Currently `_diff_reporter` directly reads `jira_fields.get("reporter").get("accountId")` — a Jira-specific nested structure. After the plan canonicalizes `jira_fields` in step 2, `jira_fields.get("reporter")` on the canonical snapshot may return nothing (current `_map_jira_to_local_fields` does not emit `reporter`). Step 4 mentions 'reporter identity aliases' are added to `InboundMapper` output, but neither the canonical key name for reporter nor the complete `_diff_reporter` replacement mechanism is stated, leaving a potential residual vendor read in core that would violate AC2.
findings.location: Approach §4 (reporter identity aliases); Approach §7 (_diff_reporter scope); AC2
scenarios: After step 2 canonicalizes the snapshot, if `reporter` is included in InboundMapper output under a canonical key (e.g. `"reporter_account_id"`), but `_diff_reporter` still reads `jira_fields.get("reporter").get("accountId")` from the canonical dict, the lookup returns None → reporter never diffs → reporter convergence silently breaks
If `reporter` is NOT added to InboundMapper output, `_diff_reporter` has no canonical data to compare against and the AC2 'no vendor snapshot key reads in core' guarantee has a gap

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete, automatable proving command to AC2 that detects vendor key reads in `outbound_differ.py` (e.g., a grep-based AST or regex check for string literals `'accountId'`, `'status'`, `'parent'` in non-adapter core files), or extend the AST gate to cover key-access patterns, so the full AC2 claim is mechanically verifiable.
evidence: 'no core code reads vendor snapshot keys (status.name, accountId, parent.key, ADF) — those reads exist only under adapters/jira/ — verified by the neutrality gate + code review'
The AST gate in test_backend_neutrality.py checks import statements, not dict-key access patterns
'code review' is not a concrete proving command that produces pass/fail evidence on success
findings.finding: AC2 claims 'no core code reads vendor snapshot keys (status.name, accountId, parent.key, ADF) — verified by the neutrality gate + code review.' The neutrality gate (AST import check) verifies only the absence of `adapters.jira` imports; it does not mechanically detect if core code reads vendor-shaped dict keys like `fields['status']['name']` without importing from adapters. The second half of the verification relies on 'code review,' which is not a deterministic, repeatable proving command.
scenarios: A future contributor adds `snapshot['fields']['status']['name']` in outbound_differ.py without importing from adapters.jira — the AST import gate stays green, code review is not automated, and the violation ships undetected
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the neutrality gate in `test_backend_neutrality.py` to assert that `outbound_differ.py` contains no string-literal occurrences of the vendor field names (`accountId`, `parent.key`, `displayName` as a raw key), e.g. via `ast.walk` over `ast.Constant` nodes or a targeted `grep -E '"(accountId|parent\.key|displayName)"' src/.../outbound_differ.py` check added to the test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct Approach §4 to say `inbound_fields.py:141-184` lives at `rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` (core, not vendor); remove the 'vendor mapper' label and update the path citation to match the Scope entry.
evidence: Directory listing of `adapters/jira/` shows: __init__.py, acli.py, acli_cli_ops.py, acli_graph.py, acli_rest.py, adf.py, backend.py, comment_limits.py, identity.py, jira_fields.py, outbound_fields.py — no `inbound_fields.py`.
`adapters/jira/backend.py:21` imports `from rebar_reconciler import inbound_fields` (core module), and `adapters/jira/backend.py:47` delegates to `inbound_fields._map_jira_to_local_fields`.
The widening work in step 4 must land in `rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` (core), not a vendor file — this matters for ADR 0035 compliance audit.
findings.finding: Approach §4 mislabels `inbound_fields.py` as a 'vendor mapper' at `adapters/jira/inbound_fields.py:141-184`, but this file does not exist under `adapters/jira/`; it lives at `rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` (core, not vendor). The directory listing of `adapters/jira/` confirms no `inbound_fields.py` there.
findings.location: Approach §4: 'implemented in the vendor mapper `inbound_fields.py:141-184` / delegate `adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47`'
scenarios: A reviewer or implementer following the Approach §4 path label could look for `adapters/jira/inbound_fields.py` and fail to find it, or incorrectly create a new file there instead of editing the core module.
An ADR 0035 compliance check that interprets 'vendor mapper' as meaning the widening lives in the vendor layer would incorrectly flag the change.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'implemented in the vendor mapper `inbound_fields.py:141-184`' with 'implemented in the core inbound-translation module `rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py:141-184`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the injection path for `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` in step 5: either add `sanitizer: FieldSanitizer | None = None` to `compute_outbound_mutations` and `sanitizer=backend.sanitizer` to the `run_differs.py` call site, or justify using the existing `_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit` path directly (which is already adapter-internal but already used in the neutral characterization suite).
evidence: Search for 'backend.sanitizer' and 'sanitizer.sanitize' in the reconciler source returns zero matches — the sanitizer port is not currently passed to any differ.
`compute_outbound_mutations` signature (outbound_differ.py:405-412) accepts only `outbound_mapper`; no `sanitizer` parameter exists.
`run_differs.py:481-495` call site passes only `outbound_mapper=backend.outbound`; no `sanitizer` argument.
Current description fit uses `_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit` — a by-path lazy-load of the adapter's `adf.py` module, which is adapter-internal.
findings.finding: Step 5 says the neutral description comparison will use `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` (the port object), but `FieldSanitizer` / `backend.sanitizer` is not currently injected into `outbound_differ` or `compute_outbound_mutations`, and the plan does not name any injection mechanism for it. The current `_diff_fields` calls the adapter-internal `_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit` directly (`outbound_fields.py:567`). Without an injection path, core would have to import the adapter directly to reach the sanitizer, re-introducing the ADR 0035 violation.
scenarios: If the neutral helper calls `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` without an injection path, the implementation must either import it from the adapter (violating ADR 0035) or the plan's description-parity step is silently left unimplemented.

If the sanitizer is to be injected, `compute_outbound_mutations` needs a new `sanitizer` parameter and `run_differs.py` needs `sanitizer=backend.sanitizer` — this is missing from Scope, Step 1, and AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py` to Scope/Testing as a file requiring migration: update its `differ._extract_jira_field` and `differ._diff_fields` calls to load from `adapters/jira/outbound_fields` directly (or replace the _diff_fields call with the new canonical diff path).
evidence: Plan Step 7: '_diff_fields / _extract_jira_field / _assignee_matches / _LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE become adapter-internal; the outbound_differ.py:39-43 imports are deleted and the test-suite re-exports migrate to adapters.jira.outbound_fields'
test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py:59: got = differ._extract_jira_field(jira_fields, 'description')
test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py:97: changed = differ._diff_fields(ticket, jira_fields)
File is absent from the plan's Scope and Testing sections.
findings.finding: test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py calls differ._diff_fields() (line 97) and differ._extract_jira_field() (lines 59, 68, 107, 124) directly on the module loaded from outbound_differ.py. The plan removes those re-exports (outbound_differ.py:39-43 import block deleted). This test file is not enumerated in Scope or Testing and will fail with AttributeError after the re-exports are gone.
scenarios: After deleting the outbound_differ.py:39-43 import block, the module no longer exposes _diff_fields or _extract_jira_field; test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py raises AttributeError and the test tree fails.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_diff_assignee_permissive.py` to Scope/Testing as requiring migration: migrate its `differ._diff_fields` calls to load `_diff_fields` directly from `adapters/jira/outbound_fields` (or replace with the new neutral canonical-comparison helper).
evidence: Plan Step 7: '_diff_fields ... become adapter-internal; the outbound_differ.py:39-43 imports are deleted'
test_diff_assignee_permissive.py:78: changed = differ._diff_fields(ticket, jira)
File is absent from the plan's Scope and Testing sections.
findings.finding: test_diff_assignee_permissive.py calls differ._diff_fields() directly on the module loaded from outbound_differ.py (lines 78, 88, 96, 104, 113, 121, 131). The plan removes the _diff_fields re-export from outbound_differ.py. This test file is not enumerated in Scope or Testing and will fail with AttributeError.
scenarios: After the re-export block deletion, test_diff_assignee_permissive.py AttributeErrors on differ._diff_fields, breaking CI.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State in Step 2 the module location (e.g., co-located in `outbound_differ.py` or a new `_diff_helpers.py`) and at minimum the call signature for the neutral field-comparison helpers, so the executor has no design choice to make.
evidence: 'new neutral field-comparison helpers in core (local vocabulary only)' — no module path, no function name, no signature stated
The analogous inbound_differ helpers are not cited as a template
Scope section lists outbound_differ.py as the home but does not confirm helpers are co-located or extracted to a sub-module
findings.finding: Step 2 introduces 'new neutral field-comparison helpers in core (local vocabulary only)' but specifies neither the module they live in (outbound_differ.py itself, a new helpers module, or another core module) nor their function signatures/interfaces. An executing agent must make a real design choice (module name, function API) without guidance, which could produce an incompatible seam with the rest of the plan.
scenarios: Executor creates helpers in a new file, breaking the Scope contract; or inlines them in outbound_differ.py, making future extraction harder; or uses a signature incompatible with how outbound_differ calls them
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Step 2: 'Define `_diff_field(local_val, remote_val, field_name) -> bool` and `_assignee_equal(local_assignee, remote_assignee) -> bool` as module-private functions in `outbound_differ.py`, mirroring the structure of the demoted helpers in `outbound_fields.py:237-261`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope entry for `inbound_fields.py` to its full adapter path `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/inbound_fields.py` to match the Approach step 4 location.
evidence: Scope entry: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py`
Approach step 4: 'implemented in the vendor mapper `inbound_fields.py:141-184` / delegate `adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47`'
All other adapter files in Scope carry the `adapters/jira/` prefix (e.g. `adapters/jira/backend.py`, `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py`)
findings.finding: The Scope section lists `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` (no adapter sub-path), but the Approach (step 4) places the widening work — assignee/parent/reporter aliases — explicitly in the vendor mapper `inbound_fields.py:141-184` and delegate `adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47`, i.e. `adapters/jira/inbound_fields.py`. An implementer reading the Scope would target the wrong file.
findings.location: Scope section (line listing `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py`) vs Approach step 4 (`inbound_fields.py:141-184` / `adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47`)
scenarios: Implementer edits core `inbound_fields.py` instead of `adapters/jira/inbound_fields.py`, inadvertently introducing vendor-shaped logic into core — the exact violation the plan is designed to prevent.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` in the Scope section with `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/inbound_fields.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion explicitly verifying that the migrated re-export tests exist under `adapters.jira.outbound_fields` and pass (e.g., `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/` collects and passes the re-export test cases in their new location).
evidence: Approach step 7: 'the test-suite re-exports migrate to `adapters.jira.outbound_fields`'
AC1 verifies removal: '`outbound_differ.py` contains NO `adapters.jira` import'
AC2 verifies no core reads — neither AC covers re-export existence/correctness in new location
AC5 (`make test` green) only implicitly covers this if the migrated tests happen to be run
findings.finding: Approach step 7 specifies that test-suite re-exports of `_diff_fields` / `_extract_jira_field` / `_assignee_matches` / `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE` migrate to `adapters.jira.outbound_fields`, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies the *replacement* exists and the migrated tests pass — only removal is verified (AC1 checks no import in `outbound_differ.py`; AC2 checks no vendor reads in core).
findings.location: Approach step 7 vs Acceptance Criteria (AC1, AC2)
scenarios: Re-exports are deleted from the old location but not recreated in `adapters.jira.outbound_fields`; the test suite silently drops coverage of the adapter-internal helpers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., a short-lived feature flag toggling old vs new comparison path, or an expand-contract deployment sequence with the old helpers retained until the new path is proven in production) to the Approach or a dedicated 'Rollback' section.
evidence: 'Behavior risk, named: the old differ compares in Jira value-space (mapped local vs raw remote), the new one in local value-space...'
'The characterization suite below pins decision-for-decision equivalence before the old helpers are demoted' — characterization testing is the only safety net named
No feature flag, expand-contract deployment sequence, or revert procedure is described anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan changes the outbound differ's comparison value-space from Jira-shape to canonical local-shape — an acknowledged behavioral change — but provides no explicit rollback or back-out mechanism (feature flag, expand-contract deployment, or revert procedure) if post-deployment drift is discovered or the characterization suite misses an edge case.
findings.location: Approach 'Behavior risk, named' paragraph; Testing section; Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: A value-map that is not perfectly inverse-consistent causes silent update-suppression or spurious updates in production; with no rollback path the only remedy is a new release cycle.

The characterization suite achieves equivalence on the test fixture set but misses a live-data edge case (e.g., a Jira status value not in the fixture map); there is no way to quickly revert to the old path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Formally update the abstract `InboundMapper` interface in `_backend.py` (typed return fields or contract docstring) to enumerate the new required canonical output fields (identity aliases, parent remote id, reporter aliases), and extend the ADR 0035 §(d) amendment to name these fields so all backend implementers have a navigable contract spec.
evidence: 'Widen the Jira InboundMapper output (translation only, implemented in the vendor mapper inbound_fields.py:141-184 / delegate adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47)' — only Jira implementation mentioned
'_backend.py (InboundMapper contract note: canonical output incl. identity aliases + parent remote id)' — 'contract note' is undefined in form; abstract interface update absent
ADR amendment described as pinning the principle ('core compares only canonical shapes; canonicalizes via InboundMapper') without enumerating the new canonical fields all backends must emit
ADR 0035 exists precisely to govern the vendor-adapter seam for multiple backends
findings.finding: The plan widens the InboundMapper port contract to require new canonical output fields (identity aliases, parent remote id, reporter aliases) but specifies only a vague 'contract note' in `_backend.py` and only implements the extension in the Jira adapter. The abstract port interface update and implications for any non-Jira backend implementations are unaddressed, and the ADR amendment describes the principle without enumerating the new required canonical fields that all InboundMapper implementations must now produce.
scenarios: A second backend implementation is built or tested: its InboundMapper lacks identity aliases / parent remote id / reporter aliases, causing the outbound differ to silently treat those fields as always-changed or always-matching, with no interface-level enforcement or documentation to catch the gap.
A reviewer reading the ADR cannot determine which canonical fields InboundMapper must produce; the contract is implicit in the Jira implementation rather than in the port definition.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_backend.py`, extend the `InboundMapper` abstract/protocol class with typed output documentation (or a dataclass/TypedDict for the canonical shape) listing the new fields. In the ADR amendment, add a subsection 'Required canonical fields from InboundMapper' listing each field and its purpose, so the contract is self-contained and enforceable across backends.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test (and specify error-handling behavior in the plan) for the case where `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local` raises or returns an unexpected shape on a malformed-but-non-sentinel snapshot entry — clarify whether the update path should treat this as a no-diff, re-raise, or log-and-skip.
evidence: 'Edge: snapshot entry missing/deleted sentinels (_DELETED/_TRANSPORT_ERROR) behave as today' — only sentinels are named, not mapper exceptions or partial outputs
Approach step 2: 'canonicalizes the snapshot entry with inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_snapshot[jira_key])' — new call site with no error-handling spec
The behavior-risk paragraph names unmapped remote status (mapper returns no `status` key) but does not address mapper raises or `None` returns
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new call to `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_snapshot[jira_key])` in the update path, but the Testing section and ACs cover only the sentinel edge cases (`_DELETED`/`_TRANSPORT_ERROR`) and not the failure mode where the mapper raises, returns `None`, or produces a partial/unexpected shape on a malformed-but-non-sentinel snapshot entry. The old code read raw dict keys and silently missed fields; the new path calls an injected method that can propagate exceptions into the differ. This is a new failure mode with no test or error-handling specification.
findings.location: Testing section (edge cases paragraph) and Approach step 2
scenarios: A snapshot entry is present but malformed (e.g., missing `fields` sub-key); the old code would silently produce no diff for that field, but the new mapper call could raise `KeyError`/`AttributeError`, aborting the entire update computation for that ticket
The Jira backend returns an unexpected snapshot shape during a live run; the differ propagates an unhandled exception rather than treating it as a no-diff or a sentinel
findings.suggested_fix: In the Testing edge-cases bullet, add: 'malformed snapshot entry (non-sentinel, missing required sub-keys): mapper raise is caught and treated as [no-diff / skip / re-raise] — specify and test the chosen policy.' In Approach step 2, add a sentence specifying the error-handling contract for `map_remote_to_local` failures.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the 'neutral field-comparison helpers in core' have ≥2 additional planned consumers (beyond `_compute_outbound_update_mutation`), or document why extraction is justified over inlining the comparison as the inbound differ already does at `inbound_differ.py:170-205`.
evidence: inbound_differ.py:170-205 performs inline comparison after jira_mapped = inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_fields) with no shared helper
Only one new consumer of the proposed helpers: _compute_outbound_update_mutation in outbound_differ.py
characterization suite tests the OLD _diff_fields, not the new helpers
findings.finding: The plan proposes new 'neutral field-comparison helpers in core (local vocabulary only)' with exactly one call-site (the revised _compute_outbound_update_mutation). The inbound differ already performs its per-field comparison inline after map_remote_to_local (inbound_differ.py:170-205) without extracted shared helpers, so there is no Rule-of-Three justification for the new abstraction layer.
findings.location: Approach §2 — 'new neutral field-comparison helpers in core'
scenarios: The new helpers are one-shot code written to satisfy a single call-site; they become dead weight if the helper boundary later shifts again.
If the helpers are thin wrappers they add indirection with no abstraction benefit; if they are thick they duplicate inbound_differ's inline logic (NIH on the inbound path).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the new 'neutral membership matching' in core is a net-new implementation or a promotion/reuse of `inbound_fields._assignee_matches` (which already handles all three identity forms); if the latter, reference the existing copy rather than writing a third.
evidence: adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py:237: def _assignee_matches — outbound copy
inbound_fields.py:102: def _assignee_matches — inbound copy, already used by inbound_differ.py:187
Plan Step 4: 'core replaces … _assignee_matches … with neutral membership matching' implies a third implementation
findings.finding: The plan adds a third implementation of assignee multi-identity matching ('neutral membership matching') in core, when two already exist: `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py:237-261` (_assignee_matches, used by outbound) and `inbound_fields.py:102-128` (_assignee_matches, used by inbound). Core already imports inbound_fields' copy; extending it or referencing it avoids a third divergent implementation.
findings.location: Approach §4 — 'core replaces the vendor shape-tolerant _assignee_matches with neutral membership matching'
scenarios: Three divergent implementations of the same multi-identity equality check create a correctness maintenance hazard — a bug fix in one (e.g. a new identity field from Jira) must be applied to all three.
The 'neutral' version may silently diverge from the vendor-specific copies if the Jira identity dict schema changes.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the existing `inbound_fields._assignee_matches` to a shared core location (e.g. `_field_match.py`) and import from it in both inbound_differ and the new outbound comparison path, eliminating the duplicate outbound_fields copy as part of this plan's demotion step.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
